_meta.what: Fly-CL reproduced INSIDE the LibContinual framework (RL-VIG/LibContinual), end-to-end on GPU, on CIFAR-100 / CUB-200-2011 / VTAB. This is the project's primary reproduction path.
_meta.how: core/model/flycl.py (Fly-CL rewritten to the LibContinual observe/inference/before_task/after_task contract) + core/model/backbone/vit_flycl.py (frozen ViT-B/16). Driven by the stock run_trainer.py; NOT the authors' main.py.
_meta.server: AutoDL RTX 4090 24GB, torch 2.1.2+cu118, timm 0.9.16, python 3.10 (conda base)
_meta.backbone: timm vit_base_patch16_224, weights = augreg IN21k .npz from Fly-CL pretrained_model/download.sh (B_16-i21k-300ep-...npz, ImageNet-21k, no IN1k finetune), loaded via timm _load_weights
_meta.hyperparams: expand_dim(M)=10000, synaptic_degree(s)=300, coding_level(rho)=0.3, ridge in [1e6,1e10] via GCV, batch 128, 1 pass (closed-form, no gradient). seeds cifar=1993, cub/vtab=2023.
_meta.metric: A_bar (Accumulated Accuracy) = mean over stages of A_t, read from LibContinual's '[Batch] Overall Avg Acc'. A_t = accumulated accuracy over all seen classes after task t. Last = A_{T-1}. All class-incremental (task-agnostic inference).
_meta.protocol_note: LibContinual's own class-incremental task split is used (binary_cifar100 reads the standard cifar-100-python pickles in natural label order; cub/vtab use ImageFolder). The authors' main.py instead groups classes with a seeded random permutation. Fly-CL's closed-form readout is order-independent, so the FINAL Wo (and thus Last acc) is essentially protocol-independent; the A_bar TRAJECTORY differs slightly with the task split. This accounts for the small residual gap on CIFAR/CUB.

VTAB.protocol: B0 5x10 (5 tasks x 10 classes, 50 classes total)

faithfulness: Independently re-implemented Fly-CL (run_flycl_experiments.py) matches the framework FlyCL class per-stage A_t exactly on the same cached features (Accumulated 86.28, IN-1k backbone). Fly-CL's final Wo is order-independent, so Last accuracy is robust to the task-split protocol -- a convenient faithfulness check.
conclusion: Fly-CL reproduces inside LibContinual to within ~1 point of the paper on all three datasets (CIFAR-100 93.02, CUB 92.87, VTAB 96.16 vs 93.89/93.84/96.54). The residual gaps are framework-convention effects (task-split ordering, projection seed), not algorithmic.
